content_en:
Another example: Allah, the Most High says:

“The Jews say: ‘Allah’s Hand is tied up (i.e. He does not give and spend of His bounty).’ Be their hands tied up and be they accursed for what they uttered. Nay, both His Hands are widely outstretched. He spends (of His bounty) as He wills.)

And here, Allah says:

“Nay, both His Hands are widely outstretched.”

So, He has confirmed for Himself that He has two Hands, . described as outstretched, which means bountiful in giving. So, we must believe that Allah, the Most High has two Hands which are outstretched in Giving and Grace. However, we must

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 28 Pw! Sy sabi

try not to envision them in our hearts, nor attempt by spoken words to explain how those Hands are, nor to compare them with the hands of created beings, for Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most High says:

“There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer.""") He, the Most High says:

SA & GAG EOE ee BS GEO D “Say (O Muhammad #8): (But) the things that my Lord has indeed forbidden are Al-Fawahish (great evil sins and every kind of unlawful sexual intercourse) whether committed openly or secretly, sins (of all kinds), unrighteous oppression, joining partners (in worship) with Allah for which He has given no authority, and saying things about Allah of which you have no knowledge.’'! He, the Almighty, the All-Powerful says:

6 SSS F BG cai cat 4 Shy AG GE ‘And follow not (O man, 1.e., say not or do not or witness not) that of which you have no knowledge. Verily, the hearing, and the sight, and the heart of each of those ones will be questioned (by Allah)."?) So, whoever compares these two Hands with the hands of created beings, then he has indeed belied the Words of Allah, the Most High:

“There is nothing like unto Him. And such person has disobeyed the Words of Allah, the Most High: 45M At pis Sa} “So put not forward similitudes for Allah (as there is nothing similar to Him, nor does He resemble anything).’'7!

Whoever described them and said that they are defined in a certain manner, whatever it might be, he has said something about Allah which he knows not, and followed that of which he has no knowledge of.

We present another example regarding the Attributes, which is Allah’s Istawa (Ascension) over His Throne, for Allah, the Most High has confirmed for Himself that He has ascended over the Throne in seven places in His Book, in all of them using the Word:

Keely “Tstawa.”” And the Words:

If we refer to the Arabic language, we find that when Istawa is mentioned with ‘Ala it implies only ascension and elevation. So, the meaning of the Words of Him, the Most High:

Ci SH GN “The Most Gracious (Allah) rose over (Istawa) the (Mighty) Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty).’”"*)

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 30 PLY! OIF ssbs

and other similar Verses, is that He ascended over His Throne in a particular manner — unlike common elevation — over all of creation, and this elevation is confirmed for Allah, the Most High in truth and He is Above His Throne in a manner which befits Him, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. This does not resemble the rising up of a person on his bed or his mounting cattle or his boarding a ship, which Allah has mentioned in His Words:

WSF seb & UE AOS. Go NG alll % S

eae ws ABLE fe obo

Ke BUS CS ll Se Boss ake

“and has appointed for you ships and cattle on which you ride, in order that you may mount on their backs, and then may remember the Favor of your Lord when you mount thereon, and say: Glorified is He Who has subjected this to us, and we could have never had it (by our efforts). And verily, to Our Lord we indeed are to return!!")

So, the Istawa of created beings cannot resemble the Istawa of

Allah over His Throne, because there is nothing like Allah.

And those who said that the meaning of:

“Istawa over the Throne.”!?!

is Istawla'?! over the Throne are in great error, because this is a distortion of the Words from their true meaning, and it contradicts what was agreed upon by the Companions, may Allah be pleased with them, and those who faithfully followed them. Additionally such claim necessitates ideas that are false, and it is not possible for a believer to say something about Allah

which is based upon a false premise. The Noble Qur’an was revealed in the Arabic language without doubt, as Allah, the Most Glorified, the Most Heh says:

“Verily, We ~ made it a Qur’an in Arabic that y you may be able to understand (its meanings and its admonitions)”."")

And the wording: ‘Istawa over such and such necessitates the meaning ‘ascended and established over’ in the Arabic language; indeed, its meaning corresponds to the wording. So, the meaning of Istawa over the Throne is that He ascended over it in a special manner befitting His Majesty and Greatness. So, if Istawa was explained as meaning Istaw/a, it would distort the meaning of the words transforming the meaning which is proven by the language of the Qur’an, which is ascension, and also confirms that any other meaning is invalid.

Also, the Salaf and those who followed them faithfully are all agreed upon this meaning, since not a single letter has been related from them which contradicts this meaning. When the wording is reported in the Qur’an and the Sunnah and no explanation contradicting the apparent meaning has been reported from the Salaf, then the basic principle is that they left it with its apparent meaning and they believed in what it proves.

If a person said: Has any clear wording been reported from the Salaf which shows that they explained Istawa as meaning ascended? we say: Yes, it has been reported thus from the Salaf, but even supposing that no clear statement had been reported from them, the basic principle which has been proved by the wording in the Nobie Quran and the Prophetic Sunnah is that it ‘remains upon the meaning necessitated by the Arabic language. So, the confirmation of the Salaf will be in accord with this meaning. As for the false premises necessitated by

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 32 ee! OWS T sgh

explaining Istawa to mean Istawla they are:

1, That the Throne before the creation of the heavens and the earth was not in Allah’s possession, because Allah, the Most High says: -

“Indeed, your Lord is Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth in Six Days, and then He rose over (Istawa) the Throne (really in a manner that suits His Majesty).’"")

According to this view, Allah would not be in possession of the Throne before the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor when the heavens and the earth were created.

2. That the interpretation by our saying is correct, that Allah ascended over the earth and He ascended over something else among His creatures (rather than the Throne) and this is, without doubt and without question a false meaning which does not befit Allah, the Almighty, the All-Powerful.

3. That it is a distortion of the meaning of the words.

4. That it contradicts the consensus of the righteous Salaf, may Allah be pleased with them.

So, to summarize this type of Tawhid — Tawhid Al-Asma’ was-Sifat, it is an obligation for us to affirm for Allah the Names and Attributes He confirms for Himself, or which His Messenger #% confirms for Him in reality, without distortion, without negation, without explaining the how of it and without comparisons.

Q. 2. What is the Shirk of the polytheists to whom the Prophet #¢ was sent?

A. Regarding the Shirk of the polytheists to whom the Prophet # was sent, it is not Shirk in Rububiyyah (Lordship), because the Noble Qur’an proves that they only would commit Shirk in matters

0 ALA ‘Taf 7:54

Fatawa on ‘Agidah (Creed) 33 Badal! ig ghd

of worship. As for Lordship, they used to believe that Allah Alone is the Lord, and that He answers the supplication of the oppressed, and that it is He Who removes evil, and other such things Allah mentioned that they used to confirm about the sovereignty of Allah’s Lordship. But they used to associate partners in worship, worshipping other deities besides Allah and this is a form of Shirk which removes one from the pale of Islam, because Tawhid means according to what is understood from the word to affirm the oneness of something, and Allah, the Most Blessed, the Most High has rights which must be affirmed to Him Alone. These rights may be divided into three categories:

1. Rights of dominion. 2. Rights of worship. 3. Rights of Names and Attributes.

For this reason, the scholars have divided Tawhid into three categories: Tawhid Ar-Rububiyyah, Tawhid Al-Asma’ was-Sifat and Tawhid Al-‘Ibadah (Tawhid Al-Uluhiyyah).

The polytheists used to worship others besides Allah. Allah, the Most Blessed, the Most High has said:

My the ed ne ce we FES IES Th AEG SED “Worship Allah and join none with Him (in worship);""")

that is, in worshipping Him. And Allah, the Most High says:

clete ee ae ee ee ee ae

Ce eter = Soe 05 JUST AGUS EIT ache tit GS as ah ES 1}

“Verily, whosoever sets up partners (in worship) with Allah, then Allah has forbidden Paradise to him, and the Fire will be his abode. And for the Zalimun (polytheists and wrongdoers) there are no helpers.""*}

He, the Most High s says:

“Verily, Allah forgives not that partners should be set up with Him (in worship), but He forgives except that (anything else) to whom He wills,"

He, the Most High says:

Ki Coll asi Eats 36} Raph ae Sbice seer ‘And your Lord said: Invoke Me [i.e. believe in My Oneness (Islamic Monotheism) and ask Me for anything], I will respond to your (invocation). Verily, those who scorn My worship [i.e. do not invoke Me, and do not believe in My Oneness, (Islamic Monotheism)] they will surely enter Hell in humiliation’!

Allah, the Most st High says in Surat Al-Ikhlas: HO RT Gis Zi ne aee 1S @ ts

“Say (O Muhammad é to these Mushrikun and Kafirun): O Al-Kafirun (disbelievers in Allah, in His Oneness, in His Angels, in His Books, in His Messengers, in the Day of Resurrection, and in Al-Qadar)! I worship not that which you worship, Nor will you worship that which I worship. And I shall not worship that which you are worshipping. Nor will you worship that which I worship. To you be your religion, and to me my religion (Islamic Monotheism).””"*!
content_id:
Contoh lain: Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Orang-orang Yahudi berkata: 'Tangan Allah terbelenggu (yaitu Dia tidak memberi dan tidak menginfakkan dari karunia-Nya).' Semoga tangan-tangan mereka terbelenggu dan mereka dilaknat karena apa yang mereka ucapkan. Tidak demikian, bahkan kedua Tangan-Nya terbuka lebar. Dia menginfakkan (dari karunia-Nya) sesuai kehendak-Nya."

Dan di sini, Allah berfirman:

"Tidak demikian, bahkan kedua Tangan-Nya terbuka lebar."

Maka Dia telah menetapkan bagi Diri-Nya bahwa Dia memiliki dua Tangan, yang disifati sebagai terbuka lebar, yang bermakna dermawan dalam pemberian. Oleh karena itu, kita wajib meyakini bahwa Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi memiliki dua Tangan yang terbuka lebar dalam Pemberian dan Karunia. Namun, kita wajib

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 28 Pw! Sy sabi

berusaha untuk tidak membayangkannya dalam hati kita, tidak pula mencoba dengan kata-kata untuk menjelaskan bagaimana Tangan-Tangan tersebut, juga tidak menyerupakannya dengan tangan makhluk-makhluk ciptaan, karena Allah, Yang Maha Mulia, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Tidak ada sesuatu pun yang serupa dengan Dia, dan Dia-lah Yang Maha Mendengar, Maha Melihat." Dia, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

SA & GAG EOE ee BS GEO D "Katakanlah (wahai Muhammad #8): 'Sesungguhnya Tuhanku hanya mengharamkan Al-Fawahish (dosa-dosa besar dan segala bentuk hubungan seksual yang tidak halal) baik yang tampak maupun yang tersembunyi, dosa-dosa (dari segala jenis), kezaliman yang tidak benar, mempersekutukan Allah dengan sesuatu yang tidak Dia turunkan kewenangan apa pun untuknya, dan mengucapkan hal-hal tentang Allah yang tidak kalian ketahui.'" Dia, Yang Maha Agung, Yang Maha Kuat berfirman:

6 SSS F BG cai cat 4 Shy AG GE "Dan janganlah kamu mengikuti (wahai manusia, yaitu janganlah kamu mengatakan, melakukan, atau menyaksikan) apa yang tidak kamu ketahui. Sesungguhnya pendengaran, penglihatan, dan hati setiap orang itu akan dimintai pertanggungjawabannya (oleh Allah)." Maka, barangsiapa menyerupakan dua Tangan ini dengan tangan makhluk ciptaan, sungguh ia telah mendustakan firman Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi:

"Tidak ada sesuatu pun yang serupa dengan Dia." Dan orang tersebut telah mendurhakai firman Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi: 45M At pis Sa} "Maka janganlah kalian membuat perumpamaan-perumpamaan bagi Allah (karena tidak ada yang serupa dengan Dia, dan Dia tidak menyerupai apa pun)."

Barangsiapa mensifati keduanya dan berkata bahwa keduanya memiliki bentuk tertentu, dalam cara bagaimanapun, maka sungguh ia telah berkata tentang Allah tanpa pengetahuan, dan mengikuti apa yang tidak ia ketahui.

Kami menyajikan contoh lain mengenai Sifat-Sifat, yaitu Istawa Allah (Bersemayamnya Allah) di atas Arsy-Nya, karena Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi telah menetapkan bagi Diri-Nya bahwa Dia bersemayam di atas Arsy dalam tujuh tempat di dalam Kitab-Nya, semuanya menggunakan kata:

Keely "Istawa." Dan firman-Nya:

Apabila kita merujuk pada bahasa Arab, kita mendapati bahwa ketika Istawa disebutkan bersama 'Ala, maknanya hanya menunjukkan bersemayam dan ketinggian. Maka, makna firman-Nya, Yang Maha Tinggi:

Ci SH GN "Yang Maha Pemurah (Allah) bersemayam di atas (Istawa) Arsy (Yang Agung) dengan cara yang sesuai dengan Keagungan-Nya."

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 30 PLY! OIF ssbs

dan ayat-ayat serupa lainnya, adalah bahwa Dia bersemayam di atas Arsy-Nya dengan cara tertentu — tidak seperti ketinggian yang umum — di atas seluruh ciptaan, dan ketinggian ini ditetapkan bagi Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi secara hakiki dan Dia berada di atas Arsy-Nya dengan cara yang sesuai bagi-Nya, Yang Maha Agung, Yang Maha Kuat. Hal ini tidak menyerupai bangkitnya seseorang di atas ranjangnya, atau menunggangi ternak, atau menaiki kapal, yang mana Allah menyebutkannya dalam firman-Nya:

WSF seb & UE AOS. Go NG alll % S

eae ws ABLE fe obo

Ke BUS CS ll Se Boss ake

"dan telah menjadikan bagi kalian kapal-kapal dan hewan ternak yang kalian tunggangi, agar kalian dapat menaiki punggung-punggungnya, kemudian mengingat nikmat Tuhan kalian ketika kalian telah menaikinya, dan mengucapkan: Maha Suci (Allah) Yang telah menundukkan ini bagi kami, dan tidaklah kami mampu (melakukannya dengan usaha kami sendiri). Dan sesungguhnya kepada Tuhan kami, kami pasti kembali!"

Maka, Istawa makhluk-makhluk ciptaan tidak dapat menyerupai Istawa

Allah di atas Arsy-Nya, karena tidak ada sesuatu pun yang serupa dengan Allah.

Dan mereka yang mengatakan bahwa makna:

"Istawa di atas Arsy."

adalah Istawla di atas Arsy berada dalam kesalahan besar, karena hal ini merupakan penyimpangan makna kata-kata dari makna sebenarnya, dan bertentangan dengan apa yang telah disepakati oleh para Sahabat, semoga Allah meridhai mereka, dan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka dengan baik. Selain itu, klaim semacam itu meniscayakan hal-hal yang batil, dan tidak mungkin bagi seorang mukmin untuk berkata tentang Allah

berdasarkan premis yang batil. Al-Quran yang Mulia diturunkan dalam bahasa Arab tanpa keraguan, sebagaimana Allah, Yang Maha Mulia, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Sesungguhnya Kami menjadikannya sebagai Al-Quran dalam bahasa Arab agar kalian dapat memahaminya (makna-makna dan peringatan-peringatannya)."

Dan ungkapan: 'Istawa di atas sesuatu' meniscayakan makna 'bersemayam dan berada di atas' dalam bahasa Arab; memang, maknanya sesuai dengan ungkapannya. Maka, makna Istawa di atas Arsy adalah bahwa Dia bersemayam di atasnya dengan cara khusus yang sesuai dengan Keagungan dan Kebesaran-Nya. Apabila Istawa dijelaskan sebagai bermakna Istawla, maka hal itu akan menyimpangkan makna kata-kata, mengubah makna yang telah ditetapkan oleh bahasa Al-Quran, yaitu bersemayam, dan juga menegaskan bahwa makna lain apa pun adalah tidak valid.

Juga, para Salaf dan orang-orang yang mengikuti mereka dengan setia semuanya sepakat dengan makna ini, karena tidak satu huruf pun yang diriwayatkan dari mereka yang bertentangan dengan makna ini. Ketika suatu ungkapan diriwayatkan dalam Al-Quran dan Sunnah dan tidak ada penjelasan yang bertentangan dengan makna zahirnya yang diriwayatkan dari para Salaf, maka prinsip dasarnya adalah bahwa mereka membiarkannya dengan makna zahirnya dan mereka mengimani apa yang dikandungnya.

Jika seseorang berkata: Apakah ada pernyataan yang jelas yang diriwayatkan dari para Salaf yang menunjukkan bahwa mereka menafsirkan Istawa sebagai bermakna bersemayam? Kami katakan: Ya, hal itu memang telah diriwayatkan demikian dari para Salaf, tetapi sekalipun andaikan tidak ada pernyataan yang jelas dari mereka, prinsip dasar yang telah ditetapkan oleh ungkapan dalam Al-Quran yang Mulia dan Sunnah Nabawiyyah adalah bahwa maknanya tetap sesuai dengan yang dituntut oleh bahasa Arab. Maka, penetapan para Salaf akan sesuai dengan makna ini. Adapun premis-premis batil yang ditimbulkan oleh

Fatawa Arkanul-Islam 32 ee! OWS T sgh

penafsiran Istawa sebagai Istawla adalah:

1. Bahwa Arsy sebelum penciptaan langit dan bumi tidak berada dalam kepemilikan Allah, karena Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Sesungguhnya Tuhan kalian adalah Allah, yang menciptakan langit dan bumi dalam Enam Hari, kemudian Dia bersemayam di atas Arsy (benar-benar dengan cara yang sesuai dengan Keagungan-Nya)."

Menurut pandangan ini, Allah tidak akan memiliki Arsy sebelum penciptaan langit dan bumi, juga tidak pada saat langit dan bumi diciptakan.

2. Bahwa penafsiran dengan perkataan kita adalah benar, yaitu bahwa Allah bersemayam di atas bumi dan Dia bersemayam di atas sesuatu yang lain di antara makhluk-makhluk-Nya (selain Arsy), dan ini tanpa keraguan dan tanpa pertanyaan merupakan makna yang batil yang tidak layak bagi Allah, Yang Maha Agung, Yang Maha Kuat.

3. Bahwa hal itu merupakan penyimpangan makna kata-kata.

4. Bahwa hal itu bertentangan dengan ijmak para Salaf yang shalih, semoga Allah meridhai mereka.

Maka, untuk merangkum jenis tawhid ini — Tawhid Al-Asma' was-Sifat, merupakan kewajiban bagi kita untuk menetapkan bagi Allah Nama-Nama dan Sifat-Sifat yang Dia tetapkan bagi Diri-Nya, atau yang Rasul-Nya #% tetapkan bagi-Nya secara hakiki, tanpa penyimpangan, tanpa penolakan, tanpa menjelaskan bagaimananya dan tanpa perbandingan.

Q. 2. Apakah Shirk kaum musyrikin yang kepada mereka Nabi #¢ diutus?

A. Mengenai Shirk kaum musyrikin yang kepada mereka Nabi # diutus, itu bukanlah Shirk dalam Rububiyyah (Ketuhanan), karena Al-Quran yang Mulia membuktikan bahwa mereka hanya melakukan Shirk dalam hal-hal

0 ALA 'Taf 7:54

Fatawa on 'Aqidah (Creed) 33 Badal! ig ghd

ibadah. Adapun mengenai Rububiyyah, mereka biasa meyakini bahwa Allah satu-satunya Tuhan, bahwa Dialah yang mengabulkan doa orang yang dizalimi, bahwa Dialah yang menghilangkan keburukan, dan hal-hal lain yang disebutkan Allah bahwa mereka biasa menetapkannya tentang kedaulatan Rububiyyah Allah. Namun mereka biasa mempersekutukan dalam hal ibadah, menyembah sesembahan-sesembahan lain selain Allah, dan ini merupakan bentuk Shirk yang mengeluarkan seseorang dari lingkaran Islam, karena tawhid menurut apa yang dipahami dari kata tersebut berarti menegaskan keesaan sesuatu, dan Allah, Yang Maha Berkah, Yang Maha Tinggi memiliki hak-hak yang harus ditetapkan hanya bagi Dia semata. Hak-hak ini dapat dibagi menjadi tiga kategori:

1. Hak-hak dominion (Rububiyyah). 2. Hak-hak ibadah (Uluhiyyah). 3. Hak-hak Nama dan Sifat.

Untuk alasan inilah, para ulama membagi tawhid menjadi tiga kategori: Tawhid Ar-Rububiyyah, Tawhid Al-Asma' was-Sifat dan Tawhid Al-'Ibadah (Tawhid Al-Uluhiyyah).

Kaum musyrikin biasa menyembah selain Allah. Allah, Yang Maha Berkah, Yang Maha Tinggi telah berfirman:

My the ed ne ce we FES IES Th AEG SED "Sembahlah Allah dan janganlah kalian mempersekutukan apa pun dengan-Nya (dalam ibadah);"

yaitu, dalam beribadah kepada-Nya. Dan Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

clete ee ae ee ee ee ae

Ce eter = Soe 05 JUST AGUS EIT ache tit GS as ah ES 1}

"Sesungguhnya barangsiapa mempersekutukan (dalam ibadah) sesuatu dengan Allah, maka sungguh Allah telah mengharamkan surga baginya, dan tempat kembalinya adalah neraka. Dan tidak ada penolong bagi orang-orang yang zalim (kaum musyrikin dan orang-orang yang berbuat aniaya)."

Dia, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

"Sesungguhnya Allah tidak mengampuni dosa mempersekutukan sesuatu dengan-Nya (dalam ibadah), dan Dia mengampuni selain itu (dosa apa pun) bagi siapa yang Dia kehendaki."

Dia, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman:

Ki Coll asi Eats 36} Raph ae Sbice seer "Dan Tuhan kalian berfirman: Berdoalah kepada-Ku [yaitu berimanlah kepada Keesaan-Ku (Tawhid Islami) dan mintalah kepada-Ku apa saja], niscaya Aku kabulkan permohonan kalian. Sesungguhnya orang-orang yang menyombongkan diri dari ibadah kepada-Ku [yaitu tidak berdoa kepada-Ku dan tidak beriman kepada Keesaan-Ku (Tawhid Islami)], mereka pasti akan masuk neraka dalam keadaan hina dina."

Allah, Yang Maha Tinggi berfirman dalam Surat Al-Ikhlas: HO RT Gis Zi ne aee 1S @ ts

"Katakanlah (wahai Muhammad ﷺ kepada kaum Musyrikin dan Kafirin ini): Wahai Al-Kafirun (orang-orang yang kafir kepada Allah, kepada Keesaan-Nya, kepada Malaikat-Nya, kepada Kitab-kitab-Nya, kepada Rasul-rasul-Nya, kepada Hari Kebangkitan, dan kepada Al-Qadar)! Aku tidak menyembah apa yang kalian sembah, dan kalian pun tidak menyembah apa yang aku sembah. Dan aku tidak pernah menjadi penyembah apa yang kalian sembah. Dan kalian pun tidak menjadi penyembah apa yang aku sembah. Bagi kalian agama kalian, dan bagiku agamaku (Tawhid Islami)."
content_ar: Another example: الله, the Most High says: “The Jews say: ‘الله’s Hand is tied up (i.e. He does not give and spend of His bounty).’ Be their hands tied up and be they accursed for what they uttered. Nay, both His Hands are widely outstretched. He spends (of His bounty) as He wills.) And here, الله says: “Nay, both His Hands are widely outstretched.” So, He has confirmed for Himself that He has two Hands, . described as outstretched, which means bountiful in giving. So, we must believe that الله, the Most High has two Hands which are outstretched in Giving and Grace. However, we must الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 28 Pw! Sy sabi try not to envision them in our hearts, nor attempt by spoken words to explain how those Hands are, nor to compare them with the hands of created beings, for الله, the Most Glorified, the Most High says: “There is nothing like Him, and He is the All-Hearer, the All-Seer.""") He, the Most High says: SA & GAG EOE ee BS GEO D “Say (O Muhammad #8): (But) the things that my الرب has indeed محرم are Al-Fawahish (great evil sins and every kind of unlawful sexual intercourse) whether committed openly or secretly, sins (of all kinds), unrighteous oppression, joining partners (in worship) with الله for which He has given no authority, and saying things about الله of which you have no knowledge.’'! He, the Almighty, the All-Powerful says: 6 SSS F BG cai cat 4 Shy AG GE ‘And follow not (O man, 1.e., say not or do not or witness not) that of which you have no knowledge. Verily, the hearing, and the sight, and the heart of each of those ones will be questioned (by الله)."?) So, whoever compares these two Hands with the hands of created beings, then he has indeed belied the Words of الله, the Most High: “There is nothing like unto Him. And such person has disobeyed the Words of الله, the Most High: 45M At pis Sa} “So put not forward similitudes for الله (as there is nothing similar to Him, nor does He resemble anything).’'7! Whoever described them and said that they are defined in a certain manner, whatever it might be, he has said something about الله which he knows not, and followed that of which he has no knowledge of. We present another example regarding the Attributes, which is الله’s Istawa (Ascension) over His Throne, for الله, the Most High has confirmed for Himself that He has ascended over the Throne in seven places in His Book, in all of them using the Word: Keely “Tstawa.”” And the Words: If we refer to the Arabic language, we find that when Istawa is mentioned with ‘Ala it implies only ascension and elevation. So, the meaning of the Words of Him, the Most High: Ci SH GN “The Most Gracious (الله) rose over (Istawa) the (Mighty) Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty).’”"*) الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 30 PLY! OIF ssbs and other similar Verses, is that He ascended over His Throne in a particular manner — unlike common elevation — over all of creation, and this elevation is confirmed for الله, the Most High in truth and He is Above His Throne in a manner which befits Him, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. This does not resemble the rising up of a person on his bed or his mounting cattle or his boarding a ship, which الله has mentioned in His Words: WSF seb & UE AOS. Go NG alll % S eae ws ABLE fe obo Ke BUS CS ll Se Boss ake “and has appointed for you ships and cattle on which you ride, in order that you may mount on their backs, and then may remember the Favor of your الرب when you mount thereon, and say: Glorified is He Who has subjected this to us, and we could have never had it (by our efforts). And verily, to Our الرب we indeed are to return!!") So, the Istawa of created beings cannot resemble the Istawa of الله over His Throne, because there is nothing like الله. And those who said that the meaning of: “Istawa over the Throne.”!?! is Istawla'?! over the Throne are in great error, because this is a distortion of the Words from their true meaning, and it contradicts what was agreed upon by الصحابة الكرام رضي الله عنهم, رضي الله عنهم, and those who faithfully followed them. Additionally such claim necessitates ideas that are false, and it is not possible for a believer to say something about الله which is based upon a false premise. The Noble Qur’an was revealed in the Arabic language without doubt, as الله, the Most Glorified, the Most Heh says: “Verily, We ~ made it a Qur’an in Arabic that y you may be able to understand (its meanings and its admonitions)”."") And the wording: ‘Istawa over such and such necessitates the meaning ‘ascended and established over’ in the Arabic language; indeed, its meaning corresponds to the wording. So, the meaning of Istawa over the Throne is that He ascended over it in a special manner befitting His Majesty and Greatness. So, if Istawa was explained as meaning Istaw/a, it would distort the meaning of the words transforming the meaning which is proven by the language of the Qur’an, which is ascension, and also confirms that any other meaning is invalid. Also, the Salaf and those who followed them faithfully are all agreed upon this meaning, since not a single letter has been related from them which contradicts this meaning. When the wording is reported in the Qur’an and السنة النبوية and no explanation contradicting the apparent meaning has been reported from the Salaf, then the basic principle is that they left it with its apparent meaning and they believed in what it proves. If a person said: Has any clear wording been reported from the Salaf which shows that they explained Istawa as meaning ascended? we say: Yes, it has been reported thus from the Salaf, but even supposing that no clear statement had been reported from them, the basic principle which has been proved by the wording in the Nobie القرآن الكريم and the Prophetic السنة is that it ‘remains upon the meaning necessitated by the Arabic language. So, the confirmation of the Salaf will be in accord with this meaning. As for the false premises necessitated by الفتاوى Arkanul-الإسلام 32 ee! OWS T sgh explaining Istawa to mean Istawla they are: 1, That the Throne before the creation of the heavens and the earth was not in الله’s possession, because الله, the Most High says: - “Indeed, your الرب is الله, Who created the heavens and the earth in Six Days, and then He rose over (Istawa) the Throne (really in a manner that suits His Majesty).’"") According to this view, الله would not be in possession of the Throne before the creation of the heavens and the earth, nor when the heavens and the earth were created. 2. That the interpretation by our saying is correct, that الله ascended over the earth and He ascended over something else among His creatures (rather than the Throne) and this is, without doubt and without question a false meaning which does not befit الله, the Almighty, the All-Powerful. 3. That it is a distortion of the meaning of the words. 4. That it contradicts the الإجماع of the righteous Salaf, رضي الله عنهم. So, to summarize this type of التوحيد — التوحيد Al-Asma’ was-Sifat, it is an obligation for us to affirm for الله the Names and Attributes He confirms for Himself, or which His Messenger #% confirms for Him in reality, without distortion, without negation, without explaining the how of it and without comparisons. Q. 2. What is the الشرك of the polytheists to whom النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم #¢ was sent? A. Regarding the الشرك of the polytheists to whom النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم # was sent, it is not الشرك in Rububiyyah (Lordship), because the Noble Qur’an proves that they only would commit الشرك in matters 0 ALA ‘Taf 7:54 الفتاوى on ‘Agidah (Creed) 33 Badal! ig ghd of worship. As for Lordship, they used to believe that الله Alone is the الرب, and that He answers the الدعاء of the oppressed, and that it is He Who removes evil, and other such things الله mentioned that they used to confirm about the sovereignty of الله’s Lordship. But they used to associate partners in worship, worshipping other deities besides الله and this is a form of الشرك which removes one from the pale of الإسلام, because التوحيد means according to what is understood from the word to affirm the oneness of something, and الله, the Most Blessed, the Most High has rights which must be affirmed to Him Alone. These rights may be divided into three categories: 1. Rights of dominion. 2. Rights of worship. 3. Rights of Names and Attributes. For this reason, the العلماء have divided التوحيد into three categories: التوحيد Ar-Rububiyyah, التوحيد Al-Asma’ was-Sifat and التوحيد Al-‘Ibadah (التوحيد Al-Uluhiyyah). The polytheists used to worship others besides الله. الله, the Most Blessed, the Most High has said: My the ed ne ce we FES IES Th AEG SED “Worship الله and join none with Him (in worship);""") that is, in worshipping Him. And الله, the Most High says: clete ee ae ee ee ee ae Ce eter = Soe 05 JUST AGUS EIT ache tit GS as ah ES 1} “Verily, whosoever sets up partners (in worship) with الله, then الله has محرم الجنة to him, and the Fire will be his abode. And for the Zalimun (polytheists and wrongdoers) there are no helpers.""*} He, the Most High s says: “Verily, الله forgives not that partners should be set up with Him (in worship), but He forgives except that (anything else) to whom He wills," He, the Most High says: Ki Coll asi Eats 36} Raph ae Sbice seer ‘And your الرب said: Invoke Me [i.e. believe in My Oneness (الإسلامي التوحيد) and ask Me for anything], I will respond to your (invocation). Verily, those who scorn My worship [i.e. do not invoke Me, and do not believe in My Oneness, (الإسلامي التوحيد)] they will surely enter النار in humiliation’! الله, the Most st High says in Surat Al-الإخلاص: HO RT Gis Zi ne aee 1S @ ts “Say (O Muhammad é to these Mushrikun and Kafirun): O Al-Kafirun (disbelievers in الله, in His Oneness, in His Angels, in His Books, in His Messengers, in the يوم القيامة, and in Al-Qadar)! I worship not that which you worship, Nor will you worship that which I worship. And I shall not worship that which you are worshipping. Nor will you worship that which I worship. To you be your religion, and to me my religion (الإسلامي التوحيد).””"*!
